Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Inconvenient Forum
Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOHN J. KEENAN, Esq. of Warren Law Group (Tel: 866-954-7687) for
Attys. THOMAS F. BURKE, Esq. and PENG SHAO, Esq. of Ballard Spahr LLP (Tel: 424-204-4400) for defendant New Epona, Inc., d/b/a Blair.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NEW EPONA, INC., D/B/A BLAIR's Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Inconvenient Forum.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to dismiss "for inconvenient forum" under CCP 410.30 and 418.10 is granted.

Defendant shows that New York is a suitable alternative forum, with a longer statute of limitations than California's. (See Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744.) Defendant also shows that convenience factors favor dismissal. (Id.) Both parties are incorporated and reside on the East Coast; thus, New York is more convenient for witnesses and discovery than California. Moreover, the at-issue contract provides for application of New York law.

Plaintiff's untimely opposition argues that "there is general jurisdiction over the defendant in California" under CCP 410.10, but that has little relevance to the pending motion. Further, defendant declares that it submits to New York jurisdiction.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Inconvenient Forum
Off Calendar Oct-18-2022 Continued To Nov-01-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
John Keenan, Esq. (866.954.7687; john@warren.law) of Warran Law Group for Plaintiff.
Thomas Burke, Esq. (424.204.4400; burket@ballardspahr.com) and Peng Shao, Esq. (shaop@ballardspahr.com) of Ballard Spahr LLP for Defendant.

The Court noted there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule.

A R G U E D. Discussion held re: late filed opposition. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW EPONA, INC., D/B/A BLAIR'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Inconvenient Forum is continued to November 1, 2022 at 9:30am, Department 302.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

Atty. PENG SHAO, Esq. of Ballard Spahr LLP (Tel: 424-204-4400) for defendant New Epona, Inc. d/b/a Blair;

Thomas Burke's unopposed pro hac vice application is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/RBU)
